title: Faithful: PAEFlop param-response diverges from headless FCP (worst ddb 9.8 dB)
goal: FAITHFUL-REIMPL (ROADMAP Rule 13): the TS engine impl of PAEFlop must match REAL headless FCP across its ENTIRE parameter space, not just values the 65 fixed transitions use. The DELTA oracle (fct/faithful) set each continuous param of PAEFlop to range extremes across hosts ['Movements__Flip', 'Replicator-Clones__Concentric'] and compared how the oracle vs the engine RESPOND: ddb=PSNR(oracle(theta)-oracle(theta0), engine(theta)-engine(theta0)). worst ddb=9.79 < pass 40 means the engine responds WRONGLY to a real parameter change (a faithful engine scores ~99 dB; the identity/noise floor is 99). Fix the PAEFlop implementation so its parameter response matches FCP. NO scene-signature dispatch, NO env flags (Rule 12/13): behavior = f(filter params) only. Re-verify: `python3 -m fct.faithful.driver sweep PAEFlop` must reach >= pass_db worst-ddb across all hosts. Worst diverging (host/param/theta -> ddb): [{"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "flop", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 28.25, "ddb": 9.787}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.0, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 20.657, "ddb": 21.83}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "flop", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.35, "oracle_signal": 3.929, "ddb": 27.284}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.5, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 10.332, "ddb": 27.847}]
